items.headline: US-Israeli aggression on Iran: What happened on 34th day of the imposed war
items.summary: Thirty-four days into the US-Israeli war on Iran, launched on February 28 with the assassination of the Leader of the Islamic Revolution, Ayatollah Seyyed Ali Khamenei, the military aggression continues to target civilian and industrial infrastructure across …

items.headline: As Crises Escalate in Middle East, Speakers in Security Council Urge Stronger United Nations-League of Arab States Cooperation
items.summary: Complementing a morning meeting held today on collaboration between the United Nations and the Gulf Cooperation Council, the Security Council met again in the afternoon to underline the importance of similar cooperation with the League of Arab States amidst m…

items.headline: How Israel is destroying healthcare infrastructure in southern Lebanon
items.summary: Israel's attacks on hospitals, medical centres and healthcare workers are fuelling displacement and a health crisis.

items.headline: Trump threatens to destroy Iran power plants as reports emerge of downed U.S. F-35
items.summary: The threat comes a day after he said in a nationwide address that the U.S. military will be hitting Tehran "extremely hard" for the next two or three weeks

items.headline: Trump Warns US 'Hasn't Even Started' To Destroy Iran, Says Regime Must Act 'Fast'
items.summary: President Donald Trump said the US military "hasn't even started" to destroy what is left of Iran after a month of devastating air attacks and he threatened to strike electrical power plants and more bridges in his latest warning to the regime in Tehran.

items.headline: US military vs Iran's 'arch defence': Dangerous new phase of war looms
items.summary: While Donald Trump talks about ending and winning the war in Iran, major US military assets are still on their way to the Middle East. These are the options they give him.

items.headline: UK Hosts 40-Nation Talks to Reopen Iran-Blocked Strait of Hormuz as Oil Prices Surge Amid War
items.summary: LONDON — Britain convened a virtual summit Thursday with about 40 countries to explore ways of reopening the Strait of Hormuz, the vital Persian Gulf chokepoint effectively shut down by Iran during the ongoing U.S.-Israeli war, as global oil prices climbed on…

items.headline: The Critical Commodities Caught in the Hormuz Blockade
items.summary: When an Indian tanker carrying liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) passed through the Strait of Hormuz recently, its progress was followed by excited live TV news coverage. “Its position was received eight minutes ago. It’s currently at 12.5 knots and is moving at …

items.headline: Oil Spikes As Trump Vows To Hit Iran “Extremely Hard Over Next 2-3 Weeks”
items.summary: Trump declares 'core strategic objectives met', threatens 2-3 more weeks of bombing, no mention of ceasefire

items.headline: What is Benjamin Netanyahu’s end game in the Iran war?
items.summary: The war in the Middle East is a huge political book for the Israeli prime minister in an election year – but it may come at a great cost.

items.headline: Putin Is Not Trapped: Why Regime Survival Does Not Depend on Victory
items.summary: For four years, analysts and policymakers have warned that Russian President Vladimir Putin “cannot afford to lose” in Ukraine. Increasingly, some argue he cannot afford peace either. In this view, the war is existential for his regime. Defeat would shatter P…

items.headline: Will China join Pakistan-led efforts to mediate US-Iran peace?
items.summary: Pakistan's Deputy PM Ishaq Dar visits China after Islamabad talks to broker negotiations between the US and Iran.

items.headline: The war with Iran may be ushering in a new nuclear age
items.summary: Global tensions are rising as nations consider developing nuclear weapons. Donald Trump's policies are prompting countries like Germany and Poland to explore their own nuclear deterrents. Meanwhile, China and Russia are concerned about proliferation in Japan …

items.headline: Iran Establishes Larak Island 'Toll Booth' to Control Strait of Hormuz Shipping
items.summary: Iran's IRGC has imposed a de facto safe-shipping corridor north of Larak Island, requiring vessels to submit to vetting and pay transit fees for passage through the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz.

dimension_signal.rationale: IRGC creating revenue-generating control mechanism shows regime focus on economic survival

items.headline: Israeli Strike Kills Iranian Navy Commander Alireza Tangsiri
items.summary: Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz confirmed that IRGC Navy Commander Alireza Tangsiri was killed in an airstrike on 26 March, accusing him of direct responsibility for the Strait of Hormuz closure.

items.headline: Netanyahu ICC Trial Delayed Until March 2026 Amid War
items.summary: Israeli legal officials secured another delay in Netanyahu's ICC evidence gathering round from January to March 2026, while appeal decisions continue on jurisdiction challenges.

items.headline: Pakistan's Field Marshal Munir Deploys 'Ghatak' Units Along India Border
items.summary: Field Marshal Asim Munir has allegedly deployed specialized 'killer' units along the Jammu-Rajouri border for potential Border Action Team attacks against Indian Army personnel, according to Indian intelligence reports.

dimension_signal.rationale: Deployment of specialized attack units during ongoing regional tensions shows increased risk-taking

items.headline: Trump Administration Approaches 60-Day War Powers Resolution Deadline on Iran
items.summary: President Trump faces mounting congressional pressure as the 60-day War Powers Resolution deadline approaches, with Congress yet to authorize the Iran military operation despite ongoing combat.

dimension_signal.rationale: Approaching legal deadline without congressional authorization shows disregard for institutional constraints

items.headline: Mohammed bin Salman Reiterates Nuclear Response to Iran Threat
items.summary: Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman has reiterated that Saudi Arabia would develop nuclear weapons if Iran acquires them, despite the ongoing US-Israeli military campaign against Iranian nuclear facilities.

items.headline: India Marginalized as Pakistan Emerges as Key Iran-US Mediator
items.summary: Prime Minister Modi relegated to single phone call with Trump while Pakistan's Field Marshal Munir gains prominence as mediator between Washington and Tehran, highlighting India's diminished regional influence.

dimension_signal.rationale: Being sidelined in favor of Pakistan challenges Modi's self-image as regional leader

items.headline: Iran Threatens to Expand Strikes to Bab el-Mandeb Strait
items.summary: Iran's Parliament Speaker Mohammad-Bagher Ghalibaf signaled potential expansion of conflict to the Bab el-Mandeb Strait, while military sources confirm Iran could open new front there if attacked.

dimension_signal.rationale: Threatening to open new fronts shows escalatory risk-taking behavior under pressure

items.headline: US Central Command Reports Iranian Mine-Laying Ships Destroyed
items.summary: US CENTCOM confirmed that 16 Iranian mine-laying vessels were attacked in the Strait of Hormuz on March 10, following President Trump's warnings against Iranian mining operations.

items.headline: Tehran Protests Against New Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei Continue
items.summary: Video footage from Tehran neighborhoods shows continued chanting against newly appointed Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei, with voices calling 'Death to Mojtaba' echoing through residential areas on March 8.

dimension_signal.rationale: Domestic protests against new leader indicate declining regime legitimacy and survival prospects

items.headline: Saudi Arabia Pinning Hopes on Pakistan-Led Iran Mediation Efforts
items.summary: Saudi Arabia is awaiting the outcome of Pakistan-led diplomatic mediation efforts before considering military action against Iran, with the kingdom prioritizing diplomatic breakthrough to end the conflict.

items.headline: Iran's Internet Blackout Enters 35th Day with 1% Connectivity
items.summary: Iran's internet blackout continues with connectivity at about 1% of normal levels on day 35, leaving the Iranian public largely cut off from external communications, according to NetBlocks monitoring.
